== kernel ==

== tools ==
- [ ] Extract and initialize segments a bit more carefully

== bootloader ==
- [ ] setup page map kernel side
- [ ] draw text
- [ ] bios
  - [ ] jump to kernel entry point
  - [ ] Should we be thorough?
    1. find kernel in fat12 fs
    2. parse elf header
    3. map memory for kernel segments
    4. load kernel into mem.
    * pros:
      - gdb debugging + symbols via qemu https://stackoverflow.com/questions/34129461/should-i-make-my-own-os-kernel-elf-or-raw-binary
      - memory protection for RO/X
    * cons:
      - pain
- [ ] uefi
- [ ] multiboot

== Resources ==
* http://www.osdever.net/bkerndev/Docs/isrs.htm
* https://wiki.osdev.org/I8042_PS/2_Controller#Translation
